title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy eyes are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used as a natural remedy to alleviate itchy eyes. They are a safe and effective alternative to over-the-counter medications, especially for those who prefer a more holistic approach to treating eye discomfort. Common herbal tinctures that help with itchy eyes include eyebright, goldenseal, and chamomile. These herbs contain anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and astringent properties that soothe irritation, reduce redness, and alleviate itching. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from itchy eyes, improving their overall quality of life, particularly for those who suffer from allergies or eye strain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries to treat various eye ailments, including itchy eyes. The active compounds in eyebright, such as tannins and flavonoids, can help soothe irritated eyes and alleviate discomfort. Additionally, eyebright has natural antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that can protect the eyes from environmental stressors and infections, further contributing to its ability to relieve itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy eyes as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by inflammation. Active constituents include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids.

Astringent: Eyebright tincture tightens and strengthens tissues, which is important for itchy eyes as it can help reduce eye watering and irritation. The astringent property is attributed to tannins present in eyebright.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture inhibits bacterial and fungal growth, which is important for itchy eyes as it helps prevent and treat eye infections that can cause itching. The antimicrobial properties are due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antihistamine: Eyebright tincture reduces histamine release, which is important for itchy eyes as it helps alleviate itching, redness, and watering caused by allergies. The antihistamine effect is attributed to the flavonoids and alkaloids found in eyebright.

Analgesic: Eyebright tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is important for itchy eyes as it helps reduce discomfort and pain. The analgesic effect is due to the presence of alkaloids and tannins in eyebright.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds, eyebright flowers are commonly used in tinctures for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, providing relief for itchy eyes.
Leaves: High in therapeutic constituents, eyebright leaves contribute to tinctures with their soothing, anti-pruritic (anti-itch) properties, helping to alleviate eye irritation.
Stems: Though less potent, eyebright stems still contain beneficial compounds that can aid in the production of tinctures, providing additional support for reducing eye inflammation and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Combine 500g of fresh or dried herb with 1000ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for extraction and maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Bottle the tincture and store it in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can alleviate symptoms of eye irritation. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and mucilage, work together to soothe inflamed tissues and reduce redness. Plantain tincture can help relieve itchiness by inhibiting the release of histamines, which are chemicals that cause allergic reactions. Its natural astringent properties can also help to tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, providing further relief from itchiness and discomfort. Overall, plantain tincture offers a gentle and effective solution for soothing itchy eyes, making it an excellent alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy eyes as inflammation can worsen the itchiness and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as quercetin and luteolin.

Antihistamine: Plantain tincture has natural antihistamine properties, which help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing histamine-induced inflammation and irritation. The iridoid glycosides, like aucubin, present in plantain are responsible for this effect.

Astringent: Plantain tincture has astringent properties that help tighten and soothe mucous membranes, providing relief for itchy eyes. The tannins found in plantain contribute to this astringent effect.

Demulcent: Plantain tincture acts as a demulcent, forming a soothing film over mucous membranes, which helps alleviate itchiness and irritation in the eyes. The mucilage content in plantain is responsible for this soothing effect.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent or treat eye infections, which can cause or worsen itchy eyes. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain plant are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-histamine properties.
Seeds: Plantain seeds contain mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from itchiness and irritation.
Flowers: The flowers of the plantain plant have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them useful for reducing redness and swelling in the eyes.
Roots: Although not as commonly used as the leaves, stems, seeds, and flowers, the roots of the plantain plant also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature plantago major leaves and stems by cutting them at soil level with scissors for fresh material or drying them for later use.
Clean the harvested plantago major material by gently washing it with cool water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with a clean towel.
Chop the cleaned plantago major material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar leaving about one inch at the top for liquid expansion.
Pour 80-proof vodka or brandy over the packed plantago major material in the jar filling to the top then seal with a lid and store in a cool dark place.
Allow the tincture to steep for six to eight weeks shaking the jar daily during this time before straining and transferring to a clean dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness. Chamomile contains active compounds like azulene and bisabolol, which have been shown to alleviate inflammation and provide relief from itchiness. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help protect the eyes from potential infections, further contributing to the reduction of itchiness. Applying a small amount of diluted chamomile tincture to itchy eyes can offer quick and natural relief, making it a popular choice for those seeking a gentle and effective solution for eye irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture reduces inflammation, which helps alleviate itchiness in the eyes by soothing irritated tissues, thanks to its active constituents like chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol.
Antihistamine: It helps mitigate histamine-induced itching by blocking histamine receptors, providing relief from allergic reactions, due to the presence of flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin.

Analgesic: German chamomile tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help diminish the discomfort associated with itchy eyes, attributable to its active constituents, including matricin and matricarin.
Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of microorganisms, reducing the risk of eye infections that can cause itchiness, due to the presence of volatile oils like azulene and bisabolol.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tincture neutralizes free radicals, protecting eye tissues from oxidative stress and promoting overall eye health, owing to its rich antioxidant content, including apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins and volatile oils, which can help soothe irritated eyes and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile are a rich source of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried chamomile flowers with four parts vodka or pure grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking occasionally to extract optimal compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can alleviate eye irritation. The herb's active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the eyes. St. John's wort tincture can help soothe dry, itchy eyes, especially those caused by allergies or environmental factors. Its natural anti-inflammatory effects can help calm the eyes and provide relief from discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties may help protect the eyes from infection, further supporting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy eyes as inflammation can exacerbate the itching sensation; the active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antihistamine: It helps to inhibit the release of histamines, which are responsible for allergic reactions that can cause itchy eyes; the main active constituent responsible for this property is hypericin.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent eye infections, which can cause itchy eyes; the active constituents responsible for this property include hyperforin and hypericin.
Analgesic: It has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate the discomfort caused by itchy eyes; the active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Astringent: St. John's wort tincture has astringent properties that can help reduce redness and swelling, providing relief for itchy eyes; the active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's wort contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's wort also contain hypericin and hyperforin, and can be used to make medicinal tinctures to soothe itchy eyes.
Buds: The buds of St. John's wort are rich in active compounds and can be used to make tinctures that can help relieve itching and irritation in the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of St. John's wort contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pests.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and dry environment or using a dehydrator for several hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried st john's wort with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka and allow it to steep for six weeks.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, squeezing the cloth gently to extract remaining liquid.
Store the final herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years, shaking the bottle daily to ensure uniform extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, have been shown to alleviate inflammation and provide relief for dry, itchy eyes. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can help stimulate tear production, which can moisten and protect the eyes from further irritation. Its natural healing properties make it a popular choice for those seeking a gentle and effective solution for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy eyes as inflammation can exacerbate the itchiness and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture helps prevent infections, which is crucial for itchy eyes as they can be prone to bacterial and viral infections. The active constituents responsible for this property include the antimicrobial alkaloids and saponins.

Astringent: Pot marigold tincture tightens and tones the skin and mucous membranes, which is beneficial for itchy eyes as it can help reduce redness and swelling. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture alleviates pain, which is important for itchy eyes as the itchiness can cause discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include salicylic acid and flavonoids.

Anti-pruritic: Pot marigold tincture reduces itching, which is the primary symptom of itchy eyes. The active constituents responsible for this property include the anti-pruritic alkaloids and saponins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them ideal for soothing itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have a high flavonoid content, which can help reduce eye inflammation and irritation.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils with calming and soothing effects, providing relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in their peak flowering stage, typically mid-morning after dew has dried.
Rinse the freshly harvested flowers with cool water to remove any dirt or debris, then gently pat dry with a paper towel.
Combine 100 grams of the cleaned calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the flowers are completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction, and then strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles, store them at room temperature, and allow them to mature for at least six months before use, ideally one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains properties that can soothe and reduce inflammation. It has astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate the discomfort and itchiness caused by eye irritations. The tannins in witch hazel can help to tighten and strengthen blood vessels, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, its natural antioxidant and antimicrobial properties can help protect the eyes from environmental irritants and infections. When used as a compress or gently applied with a cotton ball, witch hazel tincture can provide quick and effective relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture's anti-inflammatory property reduces redness and swelling in itchy eyes, thanks to its active constituents like tannins and gallic acid.
Astringent: Its astringent property helps to tighten and soothe irritated skin around the eyes, containing inflammation and providing relief, due to the high tannin content.
Antimicrobial: Witch hazel has antimicrobial properties that can help protect the eyes from potential infections, as it contains active compounds like gallic acid and catechins.
Antioxidant: Its antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from environmental stressors and free radicals, supporting overall eye health, because of its richness in polyphenols and flavonoids.
Analgesic: Witch hazel tincture's analgesic property helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with itchy eyes, as it contains active constituents like tannins and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant contain flavonoids and volatile oils, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and itching in the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain phenolic acids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help alleviate itchy eyes.

Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant, also known as "witch hazel sticks," contain volatile oils and tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs at peak growth stage for optimal potency and quality.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to enhance extraction efficiency.
Mix one part chopped plant material with five parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar or container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for maximum tincture formation and stabilization.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and reserving liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, work to constrict blood vessels and decrease the production of histamines, which can cause itching and swelling. Additionally, yarrow has natural antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that can help prevent eye infections and promote healing. When used as a tincture, the herb's beneficial components are easily absorbed into the body, providing fast and effective relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in itchy eyes, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and soothe irritated skin around the eyes, reducing itchiness, thanks to the tannins present in yarrow.
Antihistamine: Yarrow tincture can help alleviate itchy eyes by blocking the release of histamines, which are responsible for allergic reactions, due to the presence of alkaloids.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial properties help protect the eyes from potential infections that could worsen itchiness, attributed to the essential oils and phenolic acids found in yarrow.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic properties help relieve pain and discomfort associated with itchy eyes, due to the presence of volatile oils and lactones.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes.
Leaves: Contain azulene, which has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, helpful for relieving itchy eyes.
Seeds: Packed with fatty acids and flavonoids, useful for reducing inflammation and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves and flowers in the morning when their oils are most potent.
Chop 20-30 grams of dried yarrow into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped yarrow with 150-200 ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a dark place, shaking the jar daily to aid infusion and extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes. The herb's active compounds, including allantoin and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to soothe irritation and promote the healing of damaged tissues. When applied topically, comfrey tincture can create a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, preventing further irritation and providing quick relief from itchiness. Additionally, comfrey tincture has natural antimicrobial properties that can help prevent eye infections, making it an effective and natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to allantoin and rosmarinic acid, helps reduce eye inflammation, which often causes itchiness.
Astringent: The astringent property of comfrey tincture, owing to tannins, helps tighten and soothe irritated eye tissues, reducing itchiness.
Demulcent: Comfrey tincture's demulcent property, from mucilage, soothes and protects the eyes, alleviating itchiness caused by irritation.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of comfrey tincture, thanks to rosmarinic acid, helps relieve pain and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
Antimicrobial: Comfrey tincture's antimicrobial property, due to tannins and rosmarinic acid, helps protect the eyes from potential infections causing itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Comfrey leaves are rich in allantoin, a substance that has anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing properties, making them ideal for relieving itchy eyes.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain rosmarinic acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate eye irritation and itchiness.
Rhyzomes: Comfrey rhyzomes are a storehouse of mucilage, providing a soothing and lubricating effect, which can help reduce itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and stems on a sunny day when the plant is in full bloom.
Rinse the harvested comfrey material with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the clean comfrey material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a glass jar leaving one inch headspace.
Pour 80-proof vodka over the packed comfrey material ensuring that all parts are fully covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the mixture weekly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate irritation and reduce redness. The herb's active compounds, including rue oil, have been shown to inhibit the release of histamines, which are responsible for allergic reactions that can cause itchy eyes. Additionally, rue tincture has astringent properties that can help to tighten and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, providing immediate relief from itching and discomfort. Regular use of rue tincture can help to prevent the recurrence of itchy eyes, making it a popular natural remedy for eye irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rue tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in itchy eyes, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and alkaloids.
Antihistamine: Rue tincture can help alleviate itchiness caused by allergies due to its antihistamine properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids.
Antimicrobial: Rue tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent eye infections that can cause itchiness, due to the presence of compounds such as methyl nonyl ketone and rue furanodiene.
Astringent: Rue tincture's astringent properties can help tighten and soothe irritated tissues around the eyes, reducing itchiness, which is attributed to its tannins content.
Analgesic: Rue tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with itchy eyes, thanks to its active constituents like alkaloids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rue flowers are used in tinctures to soothe itchy eyes and reduce redness.
Leaves: Containing active constituents like rutin and azulene, rue leaves are used in tinctures to alleviate eye irritation and allergic reactions.
Seeds: With high essential oil content, rue seeds are used in tinctures to provide relief from itchy eyes and improve overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ruta graveolens leaves and flowers fresh or dried in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry harvested material further with paper bags or a dehydrator until brittle and crumbly.
Measure 1 part dried rue to 5 parts high-proof vodka or glycerin for tincture preparation.
Combine rue and solvent in a clean glass jar, stirring gently to ensure even saturation overnight.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks with occasional gentle shaking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation. The herb is rich in silica and other minerals that can strengthen the tissues around the eyes and promote healing. Horsetail tincture can also alleviate symptoms of allergies and infections that cause itchy eyes. Its natural anti-histamine properties can help relieve the discomfort and irritation caused by allergens. Regular use of horsetail tincture can provide long-term relief from itchy eyes and improve overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce itchiness and swelling in the eyes, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help to tighten and dry up secretions, reducing discharge and alleviating itchiness, thanks to the tannins content.
Antimicrobial: Horsetail tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent and treat eye infections that can cause itchiness, attributed to the silica and other antimicrobial compounds.
Antihistamine: Horsetail contains antihistamine constituents that can help alleviate allergic reactions, a common cause of itchy eyes.
Diuretic: Horsetail tincture's diuretic properties can help reduce fluid retention and swelling, providing relief for itchy eyes, due to the presence of equisetin and other diuretic compounds.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them suitable for soothing itchy eyes.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate itchiness in eyes.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain silica, which can help reduce redness and irritation in eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense herb in quantities sufficient for desired yield of tincture.
Chop the harvested herb into small pieces and soak them in vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:3.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 months before using, shaking well before each use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy eyes with herbal tinctures is by using a chamomile or eyebright tincture. Mix a few drops of the tincture in a cup of water and use this solution to rinse your eyes. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help alleviate itching and reduce redness. Remember to always use a clean dropper and avoid touching the tip to any surface to prevent contamination. For best results, consult with a healthcare professional or herbalist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are allergic conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome. Eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis) tincture is commonly used for relieving itchy, watery eyes and inflammation caused by allergies. For dry eye syndrome, eyebright can be combined with herbs like goldenrod (Solidago virgaurea) and fennel (Foeniculum vulgare) to promote tear production and soothe irritation. Additionally, calendula (Calendula officinalis) tincture can be used to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before starting any herbal treatment.
